summary:
The Haddon Matrix primarily focuses on preventing a wide range of injuries by analyzing risk factors at different stages before an incident occurs or during its aftermath. It serves as a versatile tool applicable across multiple contexts including but not limited to occupational safety, public health interventions aimed at reducing trauma-related events, and enhancing overall community resilience against potential harm.
The Haddon Matrix plays a crucial role in injury prevention through systematic analysis of pre-incident and post-incident phases; it can be applied broadly to diverse settings ranging from workplaces to sports environments aiming to reduce accident rates and improve safety measures accordingly.
